_changelog.0.2.5: STRAT-PAR-STREAM — added BuildStreamEvent typed discriminated union (12 kinds): 6 new agent-narration kinds (agent_started, tool_use_summary, agent_relay closed + live; iteration_update, tier_result, health_event closed + reserved) and 6 legacy build-level kinds imported from compose/lib/build-stream-writer.js (capability_profile, capability_violation, step_usage, gate_tier_result, health_score, build_end — open metadata, catalog only; STRAT-PAR-STREAM-LEGACY-CLOSE will tighten). Additive — v0.2.4 specs continue to validate.
_changelog.0.2.4: COMP-OBS-DRIFT — added optional breach_started_at + breach_event_id to DriftAxis for stable threshold-event identity across WS reconnect; live emit and snapshot rehydration both compose the event id from these persisted fields.
_changelog.0.2.3: Codex review (2026-04-24) — two joinable-data fixes + SURFACE-split propagation. (a) `StatusSnapshot.drift_alerts[]` now a locally-defined subschema that mandates `breached: true`, so STATUS cannot emit non-alert axes through the alerts field. (b) gate `DecisionEvent.metadata.gate_log_entry_id` promoted to required — every gate DecisionEvent MUST carry its GateLogEntry id. `GateLogEntry.decision_event_id` remains nullable (explicit emission-failure escape hatch, reader reconciliation documented in prose). (c) Propagate 2026-04-23 SURFACE split (ROADMAP): `COMP-OBS-SURFACE` → `COMP-OBS-TIMELINE` (region ②, DecisionEvent producer+reader) + `COMP-OBS-STEPDETAIL` (step-level surface, no direct contract consumption in v1). `_consumers` updated; no field-shape changes beyond (a)+(b). DecisionEvent emitter ownership restated: BRANCH emits kind=branch, GATELOG emits kind=gate, TIMELINE adds kind=phase_transition (on lifecycle.advance) + kind=iteration (wrapping existing iteration-loop events), DRIFT emits kind=drift_threshold on breach.
_changelog.0.2.2: COMP-OBS-BRANCH blueprint Codex review (2026-04-19): added `BranchLineage.emitted_event_ids[]` to persist DecisionEvent ids across restarts — solves the full-rescan replay hazard identified during blueprint review. Made `feature_code` a required field on BranchLineage so schema enforces the 'never emit unbound branches' rule (see BRANCH resolver in feature.json).
_changelog.0.2.1: Codex review pass 2 (2026-04-19): dropped `format: uuid` from `in_progress_siblings` items (branch_id is sha1 hex, not uuid — the constraint conflicted with BranchOutcome.branch_id definition). Added `additionalProperties: false` to every per-kind DecisionEvent.metadata subschema so new kind-specific keys can't slip through silently.
_changelog.0.2.0: Codex review pass 1 (2026-04-19): closed nested objects against drift (additionalProperties:false everywhere); LifecycleExt no longer accepts unknown keys; BranchOutcome gained `state` enum so mid-progress siblings are representable; BranchLineage grew `in_progress_siblings` pointer list; OpenLoop pinned to UUID ids + append-only semantics; DecisionEvent.metadata constrained per `kind` via allOf/if-then; final_artifact.snapshot type matches its prose (nullable); layout.md Open Loops collapse key namespaced per feature.

gate_log_entry: COMP-OBS-TIMELINE (gate-event reader — primary join is `DecisionEvent[kind=gate].metadata.gate_log_entry_id` → `GateLogEntry.id`; `GateLogEntry.decision_event_id` is a nullable back-pointer, used only for reconciliation when emission transiently failed)

LifecycleExt.description: Additive extensions to the existing vision-item lifecycle object. Closed: adding a new `lifecycle_ext.<key>` requires a schema version bump and batch-wide re-approval. All fields optional (absence = that feature hasn't landed yet).

BranchOutcome.description: One session branch (may be running, complete, or failed). The unit compared in COMP-OBS-BRANCH. Derived from CC's JSONL; no new storage. Completion-only fields (ended_at, tests, cost, final_artifact, etc.) are absent or null while state != 'complete'.

branch_id.description: Stable Forge-assigned id: sha1(cc_session_id + ':' + leaf_uuid). Stable across reads; one branch = one id.

fork_uuid.description: parentUuid shared with sibling branch(es); null when branch has no siblings in the tree.

leaf_uuid.description: uuid of the current terminal record on this branch (the tip).

parent_branch_id.description: Sibling-relative pointer; null for root branches or when sibling wasn't recorded.

state.description: 'running' = sibling branch detected but tip has no `result` record yet. 'complete' = branch reached `result`. 'failed' = terminal error record. 'unknown' = reader can't classify (e.g. truncated JSONL). Only 'complete' branches are eligible for compare view metrics.

ended_at.description: Populated when state is terminal (complete / failed). Null while running.

turn_count.description: Populated when state=complete. Null while running.

files_touched.description: Populated when state=complete. Null while running.

tests.description: Populated when state=complete. Null while running.

cost.description: Populated when state=complete. Null while running.

final_artifact.description: Pointer to the artifact the branch produced (design.md, plan.md, diff bundle, etc). Used for side-by-side diff via existing ArtifactDiff. Null while running; may remain null on complete when no artifact was produced.

snapshot.description: Inline content snapshot; null means lazy-load via `path`.

drift_axes_snapshot.description: Populated by COMP-OBS-DRIFT once it lands. Null until then — readers MUST tolerate absence.

open_loops_produced.description: Ids of OpenLoop entries this branch created. Join key into lifecycle_ext.open_loops.

BranchLineage.description: Per-feature index of known branches — populated incrementally by the COMP-OBS-BRANCH reader.

branches.description: All detected branches, running + complete + failed. Compare view filters by state.

in_progress_siblings.description: branch_ids (sha1 hex, NOT uuid — see BranchOutcome.branch_id) of currently-running siblings. Compare view uses this to render 'X of N branches ready' and disable compare button until at least two are complete.

emitted_event_ids.description: Ids of DecisionEvents already broadcast for this feature's forks. Persisted to survive restarts; consulted before each broadcast so full rescans don't replay history.

OpenLoop.description: Structured deferred/blocked/open-question item with mechanical TTL. `open_loops[]` is append-only: a loop is closed by setting `resolution` in-place, never by deletion. Ids are UUID v4 so concurrent writers (BRANCH reader + manual CLI + future automation) cannot collide.

id.description: UUID v4 generated at creation time. Collision-free across concurrent writers.

parent_feature.description: feature code that owns this loop

parent_branch.description: branch_id if produced by a specific branch

resolution.description: Null while open. Set (never replaced with null) when loop closes.

ttl_days.description: Flag for review/archive when (now - created_at) > ttl_days and resolution is null

DriftAxis.description: One mechanical drift ratio. Server-computed, no LLM in v1.

threshold.description: Warning threshold; null = no alert configured

explanation.description: One sentence describing what numerator and denominator mean, surfaced on hover in status expansion

breach_started_at.description: ISO timestamp of the rising-edge breach. Set when breached flips false→true; preserved while breached; cleared when breached flips true→false. Used to compose stable DecisionEvent ids on rehydrate.

breach_event_id.description: Persisted DecisionEvent id for the rising-edge breach. Equals driftThresholdDecisionEventId(featureCode, axis_id, breach_started_at). Reset to null when breached flips back to false.

StatusSnapshot.description: Input to the one-sentence status line. Server rolls this into prose at /lifecycle/status.

drift_alerts.description: Breached axes only — schema enforces `breached: true` so STATUS cannot emit non-alert axes through this field.

DecisionEvent.description: One entry on the feature-level decision timeline strip. Coarser than step events. `metadata` shape is constrained per `kind` — emitters must supply the minimum required payload below, consumers may rely on it.

metadata.description: Per-kind shape enforced via allOf below. Each kind's subschema is closed (`additionalProperties: false`); extending a kind requires adding the field to the subschema in a schema version bump, not emitting it ad-hoc.

gate_log_entry_id.description: MUST match a GateLogEntry.id. The join is mandatory on the DecisionEvent side; GateLogEntry.decision_event_id stays nullable only to cover transient emission-failure (reader reconciles via gate_id + timestamp ±5s).

BuildStreamEvent.description: Typed event streamed from stratum-mcp producers (parallel_dispatch tasks, build orchestration) to consumers (compose cockpit). Discriminated by `kind` with per-kind metadata subschema. STRAT-PAR-STREAM v1 emits the first 3 kinds (agent_started, tool_use_summary, agent_relay) live from Python connectors; the next 3 (iteration_update, tier_result, health_event) are reserved schema-only; the last 6 (capability_*, step_usage, gate_tier_result, health_score, build_end) are imported from compose/lib/build-stream-writer.js with open metadata for catalog completeness — STRAT-PAR-STREAM-LEGACY-CLOSE will trace emit sites and tighten.

task_id.description: Present iff event originated inside a parallel_dispatch scope. seq is per-(flow_id, step_id, task_id) when set; per-(flow_id, step_id) when null.

_status.description: Documentation tag — not enforced. live: v1 emitter exists. reserved: schema-only, no v1 emitter. legacy_open: imported from BuildStreamWriter, metadata not yet closed.

GateLogEntry.description: One gate invocation record. v1 exposed only via `compose gates report` CLI. Every entry SHOULD emit a matching DecisionEvent (kind='gate') whose metadata.gate_log_entry_id equals this entry's id.

decision_event_id.description: Join key — id of the emitted DecisionEvent. Null ONLY if emission transiently failed; readers MUST reconcile via gate_id + timestamp (±5s window). The reverse edge (`DecisionEvent[kind=gate].metadata.gate_log_entry_id`) is schema-required, so every emitted gate event is trivially re-joinable.
